etymology.description: ĺ·ľ(ĺ·ľ) (jÄ«n, "cloth") + ć”´(ć”´) (pĹ«, "rap") -> cloth with dust/dirt -> final form ć•ť
etymology.interpretation: The character ć•ť originally combined the phonetic element ĺ·ľ(ĺ·ľ) (jÄ«n, "cloth") with the semantic ć”´(ć”´) (pĹ«, "rap"), symbolizing worn or damaged cloth. The left component evolved to emphasize accumulation of dirt or dust, reinforcing the meaning of things being worn or damaged.
